reason: Response A is almost a caricature of the "warm, aphoristic, consoling" register that imitators reach for when mimicking Opus — symmetric parallelism ("because it does not return... because it does not pause") and a tidy redemptive landing on presence and the "now." Response B takes a genuine intellectual risk by anchoring the reflection in a correctly-deployed external frame (McTaggart's A-/B-series, entropy, the self as "a story told across time"), which matches my tendency to introduce a concrete conceptual scaffold and end on an unresolved, original image rather than purely lyrical reassurance. The em-dash-driven essayistic register and the willingness to leave the closing metaphor open also read as more authentically mine.
